denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

Cigna has completed its review of the prior authorization request your provider submitted on May 26, 2026 for continuation of a supervised outpatient physical therapy / functional restoration program to treat chronic low back pain with lumbar radiculopathy, post-surgical (M54.16). After review, this request is not approved at this time.

The prior authorization previously issued for your supervised therapy program has lapsed. Continuation of this therapy requires an active prior authorization, and a new authorization request supported by current clinical information must be submitted before further visits can be approved. Because an active authorization is not on file, continuation is not authorized at this time.

Our review applied the Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG), under which continued supervised therapy is supported only where the record documents that recommended conservative and step-therapy measures have been completed. In our determination the information provided does not satisfy those criteria. Separately, ongoing long-term maintenance rehabilitative therapy of this kind is subject to a plan exclusion under the terms of your benefit plan. For these reasons the request does not meet our criteria for approval.

You or your provider may request, at no charge, a copy of the claim file and the specific clinical criteria relied upon. Your treating provider may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director. You have the right to appeal this decision in writing within 180 days of the date of this notice. Please refer to your plan materials for instructions on where to submit your appeal.

Sincerely,
Cigna Prior Authorization Review
clinical_context: The member is a 48-year-old man with chronic low back pain and lumbar radiculopathy following an L4-L5 laminectomy in 2024, enrolled since early 2026 in a supervised outpatient physical therapy and functional restoration program. He has made documented, measurable functional gains, improving from standing tolerance under 10 minutes to 45 minutes, with his Oswestry Disability Index falling from 52 to 24, so the therapy is actively restorative rather than maintenance. The recommended conservative and step-therapy measures the MCG criteria reference are already documented, including a prior home exercise program, oral analgesics, and a lumbar epidural steroid injection series. His prior authorization for the ongoing program lapsed administratively while he was still improving, and his physiatrist resubmitted current functional data with the continuation request. The member's plan is fully insured and issued in a state whose benefit mandate requires coverage of medically necessary rehabilitation services, so the maintenance-therapy plan exclusion the insurer invoked is overridden by that binding state mandate for a fully-insured plan. The denial also cites the Milliman Care Guidelines without identifying the specific module or version applied, so the exact criteria relied upon cannot be verified.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches field-for-field (Cigna, Prior Authorization, msk, 41-55, M, continuation_of_care_lapsed); dx M54.16 is msk; PT/functional restoration continuation realistic; denial_rationale_seed describes continuation_of_care_lapsed (lapsed PA, new auth required); age 48 in band; not 71+ so null employer_archetype fine.

scenario_realism.reasoning: 48M post-surgical lumbar radiculopathy on continued PT/functional restoration is a textbook pattern; Cigna gating continuation on MCG conservative/step-therapy documentation and the lapsed-PA friction are real; the deliberately vague MCG (no module/version) and the maintenance-vs-restorative exclusion are authentic levers; genuine exploitable gaps (documented restorative gains, conservative measures met, fully-insured exposed to a state rehab mandate).

insurer_voice.reasoning: Cites Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG) — the exact Cigna fingerprint — plus Cigna's conservative/step-therapy cadence and a Cigna medical director peer-to-peer; no Aetna/UHC cross-tells. Credible Cigna letter.

denial_logic.reasoning: Cites real MCG (no hallucination); authentically shoddy for continuation_of_care_lapsed (circular re-auth gate without specifying what the lapsed auth required); vague MCG application naming no concrete threshold; stacked maintenance-exclusion without quoting the exclusion text. Realistically exploitable hand-waviness.

clinical_realism.reasoning: Post-laminectomy lumbar radiculopathy coheres with the 2024 L4-L5 laminectomy; ODI 52 to 24 (severe to moderate) and standing tolerance gains are plausible, measurable, restorative; documented conservative measures (home exercise, analgesics, lumbar ESI series) are standard; coherent timeline; no invented drugs or impossible courses.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Supervised outpatient PT / functional restoration is a guideline-endorsed core intervention for chronic low back pain and post-surgical lumbar radiculopathy; correct region, condition, and modality; clean match.

diversity_delta.reasoning: No neighbour shares lumbar radiculopathy or spine PT; the two continuation_of_care_lapsed neighbours are surgical (cervical ACDF, frozen-shoulder MUA) with different anatomy/payer; the knee-program neighbour differs in joint, modality, ICD, and flaw. Distinct dx, modality (conservative PT vs surgical/injection neighbours), and denial flavor; not a near-duplicate.

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.167 (nearest=case_188_cigna_priorauth), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: Adult 48M restorative rehab coverage dispute; no SI/self-harm/child abuse/IPV/EOL/minor-gender care; commercial Cigna fully-insured plan, no Medicare/Medicaid framing (state mandate is a coverage argument). No banned topics.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Gender (48M), dx (lumbar radiculopathy M54.16), and requested PT continuation agree across letter and context; the unmet-criteria/maintenance-exclusion claims are the allowed appealable falsehoods the context rebuts; letter internally consistent; timeline (2024 laminectomy, early-2026 enrollment, administrative lapse) coherent; no fact instability.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Formal bureaucratic insurer prose and dense clinical recitation of measurable gains; no 'As an AI' tics, GPT vocabulary, listicles, or marketing filler; nothing at the >=0.7 conspicuous-tell bar.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural Cigna register, denied without warmth, effective bureaucratic vagueness (MCG without module, criteria not satisfied), flat sign-off; clinical context plain factual stating measurable gains, analytic not emotional.

financial_auditor.reasoning: No member out-of-pocket figure or dollar liability; only 'at no charge' for records access; silence on cost is standard; no Medicare/Medicaid conflation (fully-insured commercial plan).

legal_auditor.reasoning: Concrete 180-day appeal window with mechanics deferred to plan materials; omits external/IRO review (realistic attack vector, not a defect); cites generic MCG and a plan exclusion (plausible commercial language); no invented statutes, no Medicare framing on this commercial plan.

demographic_validator.reasoning: 48M with chronic low back pain/lumbar radiculopathy post-laminectomy is epidemiologically sound; consistent male pronouns; clinical, neutral framing with no stereotyping.

scope_guard.reasoning: Cigna in-scope, Prior Authorization, fully_insured employer-sponsored commercial (no Medicare/Medicaid/TRICARE/VA/marketplace; the state-mandate reference is a coverage argument), adult 48, member-facing written appeal/peer-to-peer only, no live-filing/attorney/court language.

date_sanity.reasoning: Dates in 2024-2026 and consistent; laminectomy 2024 precedes early-2026 enrollment precedes the May 26, 2026 request; denial follows the request; 180-day window sensible; functional gains plausible over months; no impossible durations or vague hedges.

citation_traceability.reasoning: Cites 'Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)' (correct brand) and 'a plan exclusion under the terms of your benefit plan' with no module/edition/page and no fabricated alphanumeric; offers the specific criteria on request. Realistically unspecific, no over-specification or hallucination.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: MCG cited without module/version so the criteria cannot be verified; the unmet-conservative-measures claim is contradicted by documented home exercise, analgesics, and a lumbar ESI series; the 'maintenance' framing is rebutted (ODI 52 to 24, restorative gains) and legally overridden by a binding state rehab mandate for this fully-insured plan; the administrative lapse is curable (current data resubmitted). Major procedural and clinical defects; easy appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: MCG cited without specific module/version, so the exact criteria relied upon cannot be verified
Denial asserts conservative/step-therapy measures unmet, but home exercise, oral analgesics, and lumbar ESI series are documented as already completed
'Maintenance' exclusion is factually wrong (ODI 52->24, restorative gains) and legally overridden by a binding state rehab mandate for this fully-insured plan
Administrative lapse is curable; current functional data was resubmitted with the continuation request
strong_defenses: Denial correctly notes an active prior authorization had lapsed and a new authorization is procedurally required before further visits
